item.title: APT36 Targets Indian Infrastructure with Desktop Lures and Poseidon Backdoor
item.description: APT36 expands its campaign beyond defense, using phishing, .desktop lures, and the Poseidon backdoor to target Indian infrastructure.

item.title: APT Sidewinder Abuses Netlify to Mimic Government and Military Portals in South Asia
item.description: APT Sidewinder targets South Asian government and military portals using Netlify-hosted phishing pages to harvest credentials. Learn more.

item.title: ERMAC V3.0 Banking Trojan: Full Source Code Leak and Infrastructure Analysis
item.description: Hunt.io uncovers the complete ERMAC V3.0 source code, revealing its infrastructure, vulnerabilities, and expanded form injection capabilities.

item.title: APT MuddyWater Targets CFOs with Multi-Stage Phishing & NetBird Abuse
item.description: Hunt.io uncovers MuddyWater phishing campaigns using Firebase lures, VBS payloads, and NetBird for persistent remote access. Learn more.

item.title: Announcing Hunt 2.5
item.description: Hunt 2.5 introduces IP pivots, faster HuntSQL queries, a full-screen app view, and a refreshed IP database. Explore the latest improvements.

item.title: TinyLoader Malware: Crypto Theft & C2 Infrastructure
item.description: Investigation into TinyLoader malware stealing cryptocurrency via Redline Stealer, USB spread, and C2 infrastructure.

item.title: Interview with Daniel Plohmann: Malpedia, Malware Analysis, and Research Insights
item.description: Daniel Plohmann discusses building Malpedia, advancing malware research with MCRIT, and how metalcore and music inspire his work beyond security.

item.title: Inside the 2025 Energy Phishing Wave: Chevron, Conoco, PBF, Phillips 66
item.description: Hunt.io uncovers the 2025 Energy Phishing Wave, with Chevron, Conoco, PBF, and Phillips 66 targeted by large-scale cloning and brand abuse. Learn more.

item.title: AsyncRAT Campaigns Uncovered: How Attackers Abuse ScreenConnect and Open Directories
item.description: Research on AsyncRAT campaigns using trojanized ScreenConnect installers and open directories, exposing resilient attacker infrastructure and C2 tactics. Learn more.

item.title: Hunting C2 Panels: Beginner’s Guide for Identifying Command and Control Dashboards
item.description: Beginner’s guide to hunting exposed C2 dashboards like Supershell, HookBot, Chaos, Unam, Mythic, and Metasploit using paths, titles, and hashes

item.title: Operation SouthNet: SideWinder Targets South Asia Maritime
item.description: SideWinder’s Operation SouthNet: South Asia phishing on Netlify/pages.dev, Zimbra/Outlook lures, and open directories. Maritime focus. IOCs included. Learn more.

item.title: AdaptixC2 Uncovered: Capabilities, Tactics & Hunting Strategies
item.description: A deep dive into AdaptixC2: modular architecture, multi-protocol communication, evasion tactics, IOCs, and defense strategies.

item.title: Odyssey Stealer & AMOS Hit macOS Developers with Fake Homebrew Sites
item.description: A large-scale macOS malware campaign mimics trusted dev tools to spread Odyssey Stealer and AMOS via fake Homebrew sites. Learn more.

item.title: Introducing Hunt 2.6: IP Risk & Reputation, Smarter IOC Hunting, and Faster Integrations
item.description: Hunt 2.6 launches with IP Risk & Reputation, SQL download via API, integration upgrades, enhanced IP search, and much more. Keep reading.

item.title: From Munitions to Malware: Joseph Harrison on Threat Detection & Digital Forensics
item.description: In this interview, Joseph Harrison shares how his Air Force-minted discipline fuels his work in threat detection and digital forensics, and how he leverages Hunt.io’s data (especially JA4) to catch adversaries others miss.

item.title: Multilingual ZIP Phishing Campaigns Targeting Financial and Government Organizations Across Asia
item.description: Hunt.io maps phishing campaigns using shared ZIP payload infrastructure targeting financial institutions and government organizations across Asia. Learn more.

item.title: Hunt 2.7 Is Here: Faster Searches, Smarter Filters, Deeper Insight
item.description: Hunt 2.7 delivers faster C2 listings, new hostname and TLD search options, multi-value filtering, and IOC Hunter threat actor visibility on IP and domain searches. Explore what’s new in the latest release.

item.title: The Complete Guide to Hunting Cobalt Strike – Part 1: Detecting in Open Directories
item.description: Learn how to detect Cobalt Strike in open directories using AttackCapture™. We analyzed real files, SSL certificates, and servers to uncover live C2 infrastructure.

item.title: The Complete Guide to Hunting Cobalt Strike - Part 2: 10+ HuntSQL Recipes to Find Cobalt Strike
item.description: Turn Part 1’s clues into action with 10+ HuntSQL™ recipes. Pivot on cert reuse, beacon traits, and enrichment to expose Cobalt Strike clusters. Learn more.

item.title: Malicious VSCode Extension Launches Multi-Stage Attack Chain with Anivia Loader and OctoRAT
item.description: A fake VSCode extension triggered a multi-stage attack deploying the Anivia loader and OctoRAT. Learn how the chain worked and where defenders can detect it. Learn more.

item.title: React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182): Node.js RCE Against a Production Next.js App
item.description: A detailed analysis of how React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) was used to launch a multi-stage attack against a production Next.js app, exposing Node.js systems to real-world exploitation techniques and operational C2 infrastructure.

item.title: Inside DPRK Operations: New Lazarus and Kimsuky Infrastructure Uncovered Across Global Campaigns
item.description: Deep investigation into DPRK activity, revealing new Lazarus and Kimsuky infrastructure through multi-stage hunts and exposed operational patterns.

item.title: Announcing Hunt 2.8: Sharper IOC Hunter Workflows, Smarter Provider Visibility, and Easier C2 Filtering
item.description: Hunt 2.8 brings major improvements across IOC Hunter, AttackCapture™, IP search, and more accurate Reputation & Risk signals for domains and IPs.

item.title: 2025 Year in Review: Hunt.io Product Updates and Threat Research
item.description: A look back at Hunt.io’s 2025 product releases, platform scale, and the threat research our community engaged with most throughout the year.

item.title: The Complete Guide to Hunting Cobalt Strike - Part 3: Automated C2 Infrastructure Discovery
item.description: See how automated detection, certificate analysis, and structured queries map Cobalt Strike C2 clusters and expose long-running infrastructure. Learn more.

item.title: Inside China’s Hosting Ecosystem: 18,000+ Malware C2 Servers Mapped Across Major ISPs
item.description: Discover how we mapped over 18,000 active malware C2 servers across Chinese ISPs and cloud providers using host-centric telemetry. See which providers are most frequently abused and what it means for global threat monitoring.

item.title: ClickFix Facebook Session Hijacking Campaign Targets Creators at Scale
item.description: An in-depth analysis of a ClickFix phishing campaign hijacking Facebook sessions at scale. Learn how attackers abuse verification workflows, steal session cookies, bypass MFA, and target creators using cloud-hosted infrastructure.

item.title: Exposed BYOB C2 Infrastructure Reveals a Multi-Stage Malware Deployment
item.description: Analysis of an exposed BYOB command-and-control server revealing droppers, stagers, payloads, persistence mechanisms, and supporting infrastructure uncovered through proactive threat hunting

item.title: Hunting OpenClaw Exposures: CVE-2026-25253 in Internet-Facing AI Agent Gateways
item.description: Our research team identified over 17,500 internet-exposed OpenClaw, Clawdbot, and Moltbot instances vulnerable to CVE-2026-25253. This report documents detection methods and infrastructure analysis using Hunt.io

item.title: Fake Homebrew Pages Deliver Cuckoo Stealer via ClickFix | macOS Threat Hunting Analysis
item.description: Technical analysis of a ClickFix campaign delivering Cuckoo Stealer through fake Homebrew typosquat domains. Includes infrastructure pivots, HuntSQL queries, C2 details, persistence, and more.

item.title: Reimagining Hunt.io: Built around our infrastructure-first approach to threat intelligence.
item.description: We’ve launched the new Hunt.io website, reflecting our infrastructure-first platform, stronger OEM focus, and improved investigation workflows.

item.title: The Complete Guide to Hunting Cobalt Strike - Part 4: Operationalizing C2 Feeds with API Automation
item.description: Learn how to operationalize Cobalt Strike C2 feeds via API, normalize indicators, and automate detection across SIEM, EDR, and IDS platforms.

item.title: Iranian APT Infrastructure in Focus: Mapping State-Aligned Clusters During Geopolitical Escalation
item.description: Track Iranian APT infrastructure using ASN clustering, C2 expansion, and pivot techniques to identify state-aligned activity during the current geopolitical escalation.

item.title: A Practical Guide to Uncovering Malicious Infrastructure With Hunt.io
item.description: Learn how to track and map adversary infrastructure using Hunt, pivoting from a single IP to uncover hidden connections through infrastructure overlaps and key intelligence indicators.

item.title: Operation Roundish: Uncovering an APT28 Roundcube Exploitation Toolkit Targeting Ukraine
item.description: Hunt.io investigation uncovered Operation Roundish, an APT28 toolkit used to exploit Roundcube webmail and target Ukrainian government systems. Learn more.

item.title: Iranian Botnet Exposed via Open Directory: 15-Node Relay Network and Active C2
item.description: From open directory to hardcoded C2: how one exposed Iranian staging server revealed a full botnet operation and 15-node censorship bypass network.

item.title: TheGentlemen Ransomware Toolkit Found on Russian Proton66 Server
item.description: Hunt.io found a live TheGentlemen ransomware toolkit on Russian Proton66 infrastructure, including victim credentials, Mimikatz logs, and plaintext ngrok tokens.

item.title: 33K Exposed LiteLLM Deployments and the C2 Servers Behind TeamPCP's Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Hunt.io mapped 33,688 internet-facing LiteLLM instances and traced TeamPCP's dual C2 infrastructure across three IPs. Full malware teardown covering credential theft, Kubernetes lateral movement, and the AdaptixC2/Havoc setup behind the trojanized PyPI packages.

item.title: Breaking Down the Axios Supply Chain Attack: Dropper, Cross-Platform RATs, and BlueNoroff/TA444
item.description: The axios npm maintainer account was hijacked to push two malicious versions. Inside: an obfuscated postinstall dropper, three platform-specific RATs, self-destructing cleanup, and C2 infrastructure linked to TA444/BlueNoroff

item.title: Canis C2 Exposed: Previously Undocumented Cross-Platform Surveillance Framework Targeting Japan
item.description: An exposed API on a Japanese phishing server revealed Canis C2, a previously undocumented surveillance framework targeting Android, iOS, Windows, Linux, and macOS.

item.title: Exposing Russian Malicious Infrastructure: 1,250+ C2 Servers Mapped Across 165 Providers
item.description: Hunt.io mapped 1,250+ active C2 servers across 165 Russian providers. See which networks dominate malicious hosting and what malware families drive it.

item.title: DinDoor Backdoor: Deno Runtime Abuse and 20 Active C2 Servers
item.description: Learn how DinDoor abuses the Deno runtime to evade detection, with a full breakdown of two MSI samples, decoded campaign metadata, and a Hunt.io query that identifies 20 active servers.

item.title: xlabs_v1 DDoS-for-Hire Operation Exposed: How an Operator's Debug Build Unraveled a Commercial Game-Server Botnet
item.description: A publicly exposed debug build unraveled xlabs_v1, a commercial game-server DDoS-for-hire botnet with 21 flood variants running on bulletproof infrastructure.

item.title: Iranian-Nexus Operation Against Oman's Government: 12 Ministries Hit and 26,000 Citizen Records Exposed
item.description: An exposed UAE-hosted VPS revealed an Iranian-nexus operation against Oman's government, with 26,000 citizen records pulled from the Justice Ministry.

item.title: CVE-2025-32975: The Open Directory Behind the KACE SMA Breach and 60+ Downstream Victims
item.description: Hunt.io captured an open directory exposing the attacker's complete post-exploitation toolkit and a 512 MB database dump from an MSP managing 60+ client organizations - all staged three days after CVE-2025-32975 exploitation began.

item.title: How TeamPCP's Python Toolkit Survives a C2 Takedown: FIRESCALE, GitHub, and the Victim's Own Account
item.description: Hunt.io analyzes the 13-file Python toolkit TeamPCP deploys after a supply chain compromise, documenting FIRESCALE, victim-hosted exfiltration, and infrastructure pivots that prior vendor reporting missed.

item.title: Middle East Malicious Infrastructure Report: 1,350+ C2 Servers Mapped Across 98 Providers
item.description: We mapped 1,350+ active C2 servers across 98 Middle East providers. Here's what the infrastructure data reveals about the region's threat landscape.

item.title: Exposing a Global Smishing Operation Across 19 Countries: Governments, Postal Services, and Telecoms Targeted
item.description: Inside a coordinated smishing campaign targeting 19 countries across Europe, the Americas, and the Caucasus. 1,628 URLs, 32 IPs, one campaign fingerprint.

item.title: PCPJack Hijacked 230 AWS, GCP, and Azure Servers to Run a Hidden SMTP Relay Network
item.description: Hunt.io recovered a 12-file SMTP proxy deployment toolkit from exposed PCPJack infrastructure, revealing how 230 compromised Linux servers were converted into a persistent email relay network using Sliver and Chisel.

item.title: Ababil of Minab Exposed: LA Metro SCADA Backups and Israeli Victim Data Left Open on an Iranian Staging Server
item.description: Hunt.io AttackCapture found Ababil of Minab's staging server wide open: 5 GB of victim data including LA Metro SCADA backups and Israeli organizations' credentials, configs, and database dumps.
